column50:
Compliance:
Tipico is prioritising AML, regulatory technology, and licensing compliance as a core strategic investment area, with a targeted completion timeline around 2027. The primary objective is to outsource the complex German regulatory framework - including AML, Responsible Gambling, and broader RegTech requirements - to a specialised vendor with deep expertise in the German and Austrian markets. While an in-house RegTech platform currently exists, the company is evaluating external solutions that can reduce operational burden and regulatory risk. They are open to vetted providers capable of managing compliance processes more efficiently within the evolving German regulatory environment.
column51:
PAM, Omni-Channel Experience:
A key strategic initiative is the creation of a unified omni-channel experience by integrating currently siloed retail and online player accounts and sportsbook platforms. At present, retail and digital operations function separately, creating fragmentation across the player journey. The goal is to deliver a seamless experience where customers can move fluidly between land-based and online environments. A major challenge lies in connecting land-based customer onboarding with digital account ecosystems. This initiative represents a secondary priority but remains an important long-term customer experience objective.
column52:
Payment Gateways & Processing:
The company currently operates its own in-house payment aggregator; however, payments remain highly regulated and operationally complex due to AML and compliance requirements. While the existing system is functional, maintaining it is resource-intensive and not considered a core strategic capability. The organisation is therefore in an exploratory phase, assessing third-party platforms that can provide a comprehensive payments solution rather than individual payment methods. Several external providers have already been integrated or reviewed as part of this evaluation.
